display.purpose: Auth hero headlines and the rare moments where the product needs emotional lift.

headline.purpose: Command bar titles, auth card titles, and page-level section leads.

title.purpose: Panel heads, modal titles, and section headings inside the application shell.

body.purpose: General UI copy, descriptions, helper text, and explanatory operational notes.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, metadata, table headers, chips, and compact annotations.

shadows.name: ambient-low
shadows.purpose: Default lift for panels, buttons, and shared surfaced controls.

shadows.name: ambient-medium
shadows.purpose: Modal overlays and heavier surfaced moments.

shadows.name: auth-shell-lift
shadows.purpose: Framing shadow for the auth shell.

motion.name: standard-short
motion.purpose: Tabs, icon opacity, and small surface hover states.

motion.name: standard
motion.purpose: Buttons, links, inputs, chips, and most state transitions.

motion.name: panel-slide
motion.purpose: Sidebar and scrim transitions on smaller breakpoints.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main action control for login, ETL execution, and primary page transitions.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral action button for alternate routes, modal dismissals, and non-destructive operational actions.

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Compact state container for health, permissions, freshness, and semantic table metadata.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Default field shell used across auth, settings, ETL selection, and governance forms.

components.name: Context Chip
components.description: Two-line metadata chip used in the command bar to show scope, role, and timing.

components.name: Active Rail Link
components.description: The canonical active navigation item in the application rail.
components.html: OverviewOperational signals
components.css: .ds-nav-link{width:100%;display:grid;grid-template-columns:1.2rem minmax(0,1fr);gap:0.8rem;align-items:center;min-height:3.2rem;padding:0.85rem 0.9rem;border:1px solid transparent;border-radius:var(--radius-sm);background:transparent;color:var(--text-muted);text-decoration:none;font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,'Segoe UI',system-ui,sans-serif;transition:background-color 0.2s ease,border-color 0.2s ease,color 0.2s ease,transform 0.2s ease,box-shadow 0.2s ease;}.ds-nav-copy{display:grid;gap:0.1rem;min-width:0;}.ds-nav-title{font-size:0.92rem;font-weight:700;color:inherit;}.ds-nav-meta{font-size:0.76rem;color:var(--text-faint);}.ds-nav-link:hover{background:var(--accent-soft);border-color:oklch(88.5% 0.022 208);color:var(--accent-strong);transform:translateX(2px);}.ds-nav-link:hover .ds-nav-meta,.ds-nav-link.is-active .ds-nav-meta{color:inherit;}.ds-nav-link.is-active{background:linear-gradient(180deg,oklch(95.6% 0.02 210) 0%,oklch(92.7% 0.028 210) 100%);border-color:oklch(85.8% 0.03 208);color:var(--accent-strong);box-shadow:inset 0 1px 0 oklch(100% 0 0 / 0.45);}.ds-nav-link:focus-visible{outline:2px solid var(--accent);outline-offset:2px;}

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: Default surfaced container for overview modules, workspace cards, and panel blocks.

components.description: Signature auth-side signal panel combining dark atmospheric framing with operational copy.

Auth GatewayResponding
Session policyApplied after login
components.css: .ds-auth-signal{display:grid;gap:0.3rem;padding:1rem 1.05rem;border:1px solid oklch(43% 0.02 220);border-radius:1.2rem;background:linear-gradient(180deg,oklch(30% 0.017 220 / 0.9) 0%,oklch(28% 0.016 220 / 0.92) 100%);box-shadow:inset 0 1px 0 oklch(90% 0.008 210 / 0.03);max-width:24rem;}.ds-auth-signal-title{color:oklch(73% 0.016 208);font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,'Segoe UI',system-ui,sans-serif;font-size:0.73rem;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:0.11em;text-transform:uppercase;}.ds-auth-signal-row{display:grid;grid-template-columns:minmax(0,1fr) auto;gap:1rem;align-items:center;padding:0.55rem 0;border-top:1px solid oklch(95% 0.01 210 / 0.08);}.ds-auth-signal-row span{color:oklch(84% 0.011 210);font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,'Segoe UI',system-ui,sans-serif;font-size:0.9rem;}.ds-auth-signal-row strong{color:oklch(97% 0.006 210);font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,'Segoe UI',system-ui,sans-serif;font-size:0.88rem;font-weight:760;text-align:right;}

narrative.overview:
The current interface behaves like a light operations room rather than a marketing surface. It is dense enough for ETL control, tenant administration, and dashboard access, but it softens the experience with rounded geometry, pale layered neutrals, and diffuse lift.

The active system is restrained, product-native, and blue-tinted. Because PRODUCT.md explicitly rejects generic admin-template drift, accent color has to stay functional and sparse: admin sees upload, ETL, scope, and health first, while viewer flows reduce noise and move toward charts quickly.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained action color over cool blue-tinted neutrals
One-family sans hierarchy with compact metadata labels
Rounded panels and soft ambient lift instead of hard enterprise framing
Standard app-shell patterns: rail, command bar, chips, tables, inline forms
Operational signal stacks that explain status, not just show color

rules.name: The Restraint Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue is the action voice, not the wallpaper. Keep saturated accent on primary actions, active navigation, focus, and meaningful live-state emphasis.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy comes from weight, scale, and tracking, not from introducing a decorative second font.

rules.name: The Soft Lift Rule
rules.body: Use tonal layering plus low-contrast ambient shadow. If a surface starts to feel glossy or glassy, the lift is too strong.

dos: Do keep Signal Blue for primary actions, active navigation, focus, and live emphasis.
Do preserve the shared radius scale of 14px, 20px, 28px, and pill geometry.
Do use uppercase metadata labels around 0.72rem to 0.76rem for operational annotations.
Do extend the active system from system.css, auth.css, dashboard.css, and settings.css.
donts: Don't let the interface slide into the blue SaaS admin template reflex by flooding inactive surfaces with accent color.
Don't default to the hero-metric template of big number, small label, and repeated identical KPI cards.
Don't reduce every state to the same pill or rely on color alone to express severity or permission.
Don't revive the legacy login.css hex palette as the source of truth for new work.
